title: Introduction
description: A brief introduction to Streamer.bot
summary:
Your Bot

- With Streamer.bot, the bot is **yours**!

Streamer.bot is a **local** bot, meaning all connections are made directly from your local PC to any configured external services, such as Twitch, YouTube, etc.

title: Installation
description: Download and install the latest version of Streamer.bot

title: Initial Setup Guide
description: Let's get you up and running with Streamer.bot!
summary:
Overview

This guide covers the basics of the following initial setup of Streamer.bot:

- Connect to your streaming **platform**, such as Twitch or YouTube
- Connect your **broadcaster** software, such as OBS Studio or Streamlabs Desktop

title: FAQ
description: Frequently Asked Questions

title: Bottles Installation
description: Install Streamer.bot on Linux with Bottles
headings: Fix freezing UI and graphical glitches.
summary:
In this guide, we'll walk through the steps to install **Streamer.bot** on Linux using Bottles{target=_blank rel=noopener}.

Streamer.bot is preconfigured via Bottles' built-in program installers, making the installation process straightforward.

You can inspect the preconfigured Bottles installer, streamerbot.yml, on GitHub.

title: Linux Installation
description: Install Streamer.bot on Linux
summary:
Streamer.bot on Linux is **experimental** and not officially supported.

Introduction
Streamer.bot can be installed on Linux using wine and winetricks

- wine >= 6.19{lang=bash}
- winetricks >= 20210206{lang=bash}
  - dotnet48
  - d3dcompiler_47
  - dxvk
  - corefonts *optional*
    - May be needed if you have issues launching Streamer.bot
  - sapi *optional*
    - Needed for Speaker.bot support

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
sudo apt install winetricks
          sudo winetricks --self-update

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code: sudo apt install git curl

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
# Install wine
        sudo pacman -S wine

        # Install winetricks
        sudo pacman -S winetricks

        # Install remaining dependencies
        sudo pacman -S git curl

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code:
# Clone the repository
  git clone https://github.com/Streamerbot/sb-linux-installer

  # Change directory
  cd ./sb-linux-installer

  # Execute the installer
  ./install.sh

title: Actions

title: Triggers
description: Overview of trigger configuration in Streamer.bot
summary:
Introduction
The release of Streamer.bot 0.2.0 included a large overhaul to the events system, with the introduction of Triggers.

You now have more control over events and their execution through Trigger configuration options, and events can now trigger more than one action.

Triggers also introduced the ability to easily test your actions!

codeBlocks.label: Register Trigger
Execute Trigger
codeBlocks.code:
// Trigger the "Game Win" trigger using its name
  CPH.TriggerCodeEvent("gameResultWin", true);

codeBlocks.label: Register Nested Triggers
Execute Nested Triggers

title: Variables
description: Usage of arguments and variables in your Streamer.bot actions
summary:
Introduction
When a trigger executes an action, it will automatically generate a set of variables specific to that event source and type, and make them available to that action via the **argument stack**. You can use subsequent sub-actions to populate additional arguments, or even to manipulate existing arguments on the stack.

Arguments only exist within the scope of the current action. Once the action finishes execution, they can not be referenced by any other action. To share variables across multiple actions, or to persist them across restarts, you can store them as Global Variables.

Explore all available triggers and their variables in the Triggers API References

title: Commands

summary:
Chat commands in Streamer.bot are **platform-agnostic**, supporting Twitch, YouTube and Kick.

Configuration options give you fine-grained control over command text parsing, cooldowns, and user permissions.

This tutorial was created using an older version of Streamer.bot. Some details may differ from the current version.

title: Voice Control
description: Execute actions with your own voice!
summary:
Leveraging the Microsoft Speech Recognition built into Windows, you can configure **Speech-to-Text** commands to execute actions with your own voice.

This feature relies on the **Microsoft Voice Recognition** engine. Check out the resources for useful information.

Commands

title: MIDI
description: Configure MIDI I/O with Streamer.bot
summary:
Streamer.bot ships with full MIDI input & output support, allowing you to execute your actions with the push of a button using your existing MIDI device, or even let your bot take control of your devices by using sub-actions to send outgoing MIDI commands.

Devices

**In Streamer.bot**, navigate to the **MIDI** tab

title: Import & Export
description: Import ready-to-use functionality from the community or share your own creations.
summary:
Introduction
Streamer.bot includes the ability to import & export collections of preconfigured functionality to easily share your creations with others, or between multiple instances of the application.

An export may include any combination of the following:

- Actions & Sub-Actions
- Triggers
- Action Queues
- Commands
- Timed Actions
- WebSocket Clients
- WebSocket Servers

title: Backup & Restore
description: Easily fix things when they go wrong!
summary:
Streamer.bot **automatically** performs backups any time you open the application or perform an update with the in-built updater.

You can use these backups to fully restore your data, in case you accidentally deleted something and would like to restore it.

You can even fix catastrophic scenarios preventing you from starting up Streamer.bot by simply restoring an automatic backup!

title: Credits
description: Configure the built-in Credits system in Streamer.bot
summary:
Streamer.bot has a built in 'Credits' system. It allows Broadcasters to display the different events that occurred throughout the stream.

Setup

In order to use the Credits, navigate to Services -> Credits and from there, check the events you want included.

title: HTML Overlay
description: Overlay a custom browser source on your own screen!
summary:
Introduction
This is a simple cross-platform app that runs in the background, allowing for a browser source to be displayed on the creator's desktop, creating an overlay on their own screen.

For example, the app comes preconfigured to show a simple dark splat that will cover most of the screen, (think Mario Kart oil spots) distracting them.

All you need to do is create a Channel Reward with the name ***SPLAT*** then relax and enjoy the distraction.

title: Sub-Actions
description: Reference of all Streamer.bot Sub-Actions

title: Clear Action Queue
description: Remove all pending actions from an Action Queue
summary:
Parameters

Select the queue to clear, or select All to clear all queues

title: Set Action Queue Blocking State
description: Toggle blocking on or off for an Action Queue
summary:
Parameters

Select an existing action queue

- Enabled - Enable the blocking state for the selected queue
  - Disabled - Disable the blocking state for the selected queue
  - Toggle - Toggle the existing blocking state

title: Set Action Queue Pause State
description: Pause or resume an Action Queue
summary:
Pausing all queues will effectively disable the bot from performing any commands, alerts, redeems, etc., until the queue is resumed.

Parameters

Select an existing action queue to, or select All to modify all queues

title: Get Action Group State
description: Fetch the current enabled state of an action group

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: Choose the action group you want to fetch the state of

variables.name: actionGroupState
variables.description:
State of the selected group
- `0` - All actions in the group are disabled
- `1` - All actions in the group are enabled
- `2` - A mix of enabled and disabled actions

variables.name: actionsEnabled
variables.description: List of all enabled action IDs from the selected group

variables.name: actionsDisabled
variables.description: List of all disabled action IDs from the selected group

title: Get Action State
description: Fetch the current enabled state of a specific action

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: Choose the action you want the fetch the state of

variables.name: actionState
variables.description: Enabed state of the selected action

title: Run Action
description: Execute another Streamer.bot action
summary: The executed action can populate its own variables which will then available to your calling action.

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: Choose the action you want to execute

parameters.name: Run Action Immediately
parameters.description:
When checked, the executed action will be run inline with the current set of sub-actions.

Uncheck this if you want the action to run independently, in its own queue.

title: Set Action Group State
description: Enable or disable a group of actions
summary: To modify the state of an individual action, see the Set Action State sub-action

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: Choose the action group you want to enable or disable

parameters.name: State
parameters.description:
Choose the new state for all actions in the selected group.

- `Enabled`: Enable all actions in the selected group
- `Disabled`: Disable all actions in the selected group
- `Toggle`: Toggle the current state of all actions in the selected group

title: Set Action State
description: Enable or disable a specific action
summary: To modify the state of an entire action group, see the Set Action Group State sub-action

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: Choose the action you want to enable or disable

parameters.name: State
parameters.description:
Choose the new state for the selected action

- `Enabled`: Enable the selected action
- `Disabled`: Disable the selected action
- `Toggle`: Toggle the current state of the selected action

title: Set Sub-Action State
description: Enable or disable specific sub-actions within an action
summary: To modify the state of an entire action, and all sub-actions, see the Set Action State sub-action

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: Choose the parent action of the sub-action you wish to modify

parameters.name: Sub-Action
parameters.description: Choose the specific sub-action you wish to modify

parameters.name: State
parameters.description:
Choose the new state for the selected sub-action

- `Enabled`: Enable the selected sub-action
- `Disabled`: Disable the selected sub-action
- `Toggle`: Toggle the current state of the selected sub-action

title: Set Sub-Action Weight
description: Changes the random weighting of specific sub-actions within an action
summary: Only applies to sub-actions of an Action or sub-action group that is set to Random.

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: Choose the parent action of the sub-action you wish to modify

parameters.name: Sub-Action
parameters.description: Choose the specific sub-action you wish to modify

parameters.name: Weight
parameters.description:
Sets the new "weight" for the selected sub-action, which is the probability, relative to the other sub-actions' weights, that the sub-action will be chosen.

For example, if all sub-action weights are the same, then they will all be equally likely to execute.

If one sub-action's weight is double another's, then that sub-action will be chosen twice as often.

title: Set Argument
description: Assign a custom variable for use by the following sub-actions

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description:
Enter the name of the variable you want to set.

_Do not add the `%percent%`{lang=cs} symbols. Those only allow you to access your variable in later sub-actions_

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description:
Enter the new value to store with the given variable name

::tip
You can use other [Variables](/guide/variables) or even manipulate existing arguments with [Inline Functions](/guide/variables#inline-functions)
::

parameters.name: Auto Type

title: Get Command Group State
description: Get the current enabled state for all commands in a group

variables.name: commandGroupState
variables.description: Current enabled state for the command group

variables.name: commandsEnabled
variables.description: List of all commands currently *enabled* within this command group

variables.name: commandsDisabled
variables.description: List of all commands currently *disabled* within this command group

title: Get Command State
description: Check the current enabled state of a command
summary:
You could use this to check if a command is enabled or disabled. This value is stored in the Boolean variable commandState and can be True or False.

!get_command_state_dialog_box.png

Parameters

variables.name: commandState
variables.description: The current enabled state of this command

title: Get Commands
description: Fetch a list of all configured commands
summary:
This sub-action is useful if you want to output a list of commands to your chat.

The Get Commands sub-action will not include commands that have their location set to Anywhere

Handling Long Command Lists

parameters.name: Group
parameters.description: The command group to get commands from. Use "All" to get commands from all groups.

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: The variable name to store the resulting commands in.

parameters.name: Include All
parameters.description: Include all commands regardless of their `Include` option checked or not.

parameters.name: Only Has Permission
parameters.description: Only get commands for which the user has permissions.

parameters.name: Ignore Aliases
parameters.description: When checked it will only use the first command alias you have defined in the command settings.

variables.name: <variableName>
variables.description: Comma-separated string containing all commands matching your specified criteria

variables.name: <variableName>List
variables.description: C# List object containing all commands matching your specified criteria

codeBlocks.label: Split Commands Example.cs

title: Set Command Group State
description: Modify the enabled state of a command group
summary:
Configuration

Select the command group you want to modify
  
  
  Select the new state to apply to the selected command group

title: Set Command State
description: Modify the enabled state of a command
summary:
Configuration

Select the command you want to modify
  
  
  Select the new state to apply to the selected command

title: Execute C# Code
description: Sub-action for writing advanced custom actions with C# code
summary: :read-more{to=/api/csharp/guide}

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description:
Optionally set a descriptive name for your C# sub-action.

::tip
`Name` is required if you would like to share custom methods with the [Execute C# Method](/api/sub-actions/core/csharp/execute-csharp-method) sub-action.
::

parameters.name: Description
parameters.description: Optionally set a longer description detailing what your C# sub-action does.

parameters.name: Precompile on Application Start
parameters.description: By ticking this, your code will be automatically compiled when you start Streamer.bot so it is ready to go, instead of the first time the sub-action is executed.

parameters.name: Delayed Start
parameters.description:
When `Precompile on Application Start`{color=primary} is enabled, delay compilation until later in the startup routine.

::tip
This is useful if you are **implementing UI** within your C# sub-action.
::

parameters.name: Save Result to Variable
parameters.description:
Save the `bool`{lang=cs} result of the `Execute()`{lang=cs} method ::warning **This changes the default behavior of the sub-action**
By default, a method returning `false`{lang=cs} will simply stop execution of the entire action.
Enabling this option allows you to collect the result and also continue execution **regardless of the return value**. ::

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: When `Save Result to Variable`{color=primary} is enabled, define the name of the resulting variable.

title: Execute C# Method
description: Execute a custom method defined in another Execute C# Code sub-action
summary:
Using this sub-action you can directly call into methods defined in any existing Execute C# Code sub-actions in your Streamer.bot instance.

:read-more{to=/api/csharp/guide/custom-methods}

parameters.name: Execute C# Code
parameters.description:
Select the [`Execute C# Code`](/api/sub-actions/core/csharp/execute-csharp-code) sub-action instance you would like to reference.

::warning
You must configure the `Name` field in your `Execute C# Code` sub-action settings to display it here. [Read more >](/api/sub-actions/core/csharp/execute-csharp-code#configuration)
::

parameters.name: Method
parameters.description:
Select the method to execute

::tip
This can be any custom defined method, or even the main `Execute()`{lang=cs} method.
::

parameters.name: Run on UI Thread
parameters.description: Whether to run the method on the UI thread. This is useful if your method is spawning UI elements.

parameters.name: Save Result to Variable
parameters.description:
Whether to save the `bool`{lang=cs} result of the method to a variable ::warning **This changes the default behavior of the sub-action**
By default, a method returning `false`{lang=cs} will simply stop execution of the entire action.
Enabling this option allows you to collect the result and also continue execution **regardless of the return value**. ::

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: When `Save Result to Variable`{color=primary} is enabled, define the name of the resulting variable.

title: Delay
description: Add a delay between sub-actions
summary:
Parameters

The duration of the delay

Generate a random delay between min/max values

title: File Exists
description: Determines if a given file exists

parameters.name: File to Check if Exists
parameters.description: Select the file to check, or enter a path

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: The variable name to contain the result.

variables.name: fileExists
variables.description: `True` if the file exists, `False` if it doesn't.

title: Folder Exists
description: Determines if a given folder exists

parameters.name: Folder to Check if Exists
parameters.description: Select the folder to check, or enter a path

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: The variable name to contain the result.

variables.name: folderExists
variables.description: `True` if the folder exists, `False` if it doesn't.

title: Read Lines From File
description: Load the contents of a file into variables

parameters.name: File to read from
parameters.description: Select the file to load, or enter a path

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: Enter a variable name to override the default `line#` variables with your own naming scheme

parameters.name: Parse Variables
parameters.description: Enable this option to enable parsing and replacement of `%variables%`{lang=cs} found within the file contents

parameters.name: Attempt auto-typing
parameters.description:
While reading the contents of the file, attempt to auto-type on a line-by-line basis.

For example, if a line consists of a number, the resulting variable for that line can be properly typed as an `int`{lang=cs}

variables.name: lineCount
variables.description: Total number of lines in the file

variables.name: line#
variables.description:
Each line of output parsed from the file parsed to its own variable, numbered from 0

e.g. `%line0%`{lang=cs}, `%line1%`{lang=cs}, `%line2%`{lang=cs}, and so on...

variables.name: fileFound
variables.description: `True` or `False` if the file is present on disk

title: Read Random Lines From File
description: Load the contents of a file and populate a set of variables from random lines

parameters.name: File to read from
parameters.description: Select the file to load, or enter a path

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: Enter a variable name to override the default `randomLine#` variables with your own naming scheme

parameters.name: Parse Variables
parameters.description: Enable this option to enable parsing and replacement of `%variables%`{lang=cs} found within the file contents

parameters.name: Attempt auto-typing
parameters.description:
While reading the contents of the file, attempt to auto-type on a line-by-line basis.

For example, if a line consists a number, the resulting variable for that line can be properly typed as an `int`{lang=cs}

parameters.name: Count
parameters.description: Number of random lines to extract from the file

variables.name: randomLine#
variables.description:
Each random line extracted based on the `Count` you configured. The line counter suffix is omitted from the first line read.

e.g. `%randomLine%`{lang=cs}, `%randomLine1%`{lang=cs}, `%randomLine2%`{lang=cs}, and so on...

variables.name: randomLineNumber#
variables.description:
The line number (numbered from 0) of each random line read. The line counter suffix is omitted from the first line number.

e.g. `%randomLineNumber%`{lang=cs}, `%randomLineNumber1%`{lang=cs}, `%randomLineNumber2%`{lang=cs}, and so on...

variables.name: fileFound
variables.description: `True` or `False` if the file is present on disk

title: Read Specific Line From File
description: Populate a variable with the contents of a specific line from a file

parameters.name: File to read from
parameters.description: Select the file to load, or enter a path

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: Enter a variable name to override the default `line` variable

parameters.name: Parse Variables
parameters.description: Enable this option to enable parsing and replacement of `%variables%`{lang=cs} found within the file contents

parameters.name: Attempt auto-typing
parameters.description:
While reading the contents of the file, attempt to auto-type on a line-by-line basis.

For example, if a line consists a number, the resulting variable for that line can be properly typed as an `int`{lang=cs}

parameters.name: Line Number
parameters.description: The line to extract from the file, numbered from 1

variables.name: line
variables.description: Contents of the selected line from the file

variables.name: fileFound
variables.description: `True` or `False` if the file is present on disk

title: Write To File
description: Write variable data to a specified file

parameters.name: File to write to
parameters.description: Select a file, or enter a path

parameters.name: Append to file
parameters.description: Append to the existing file contents rather than overwriting

parameters.name: Text to write
parameters.description: Enter the text to write to the file, this can include variables

title: Set File Tail State
description: Enable or disable file tailing
summary: To configure your file tails, navigate to **Settings > File Tails** in Streamer.bot

parameters.name: File Tail
parameters.description: Select the file tail to modify

parameters.name: State
parameters.description:
Select the new state for the file tail

- `Enabled`: Enable the selected file tail
- `Disabled`: Disable the selected file tail
- `Toggle`: Toggle the existing state of the selected file tail

title: Clear Non-Persisted Globals
description: Reset all temporary global variables
summary:
Clear all temporary non-user global variables. Effectively the same as restarting Streamer.bot.

:read-more{to=/guide/variables}

Parameters
No parameters

title: Clear Non-Persisted User Globals
description: Reset all temporary user global variables
summary:
Clear all temporary user global variables. Effectively the same as restarting Streamer.bot.

:read-more{to=/guide/variables}

Parameters
No parameters

title: Get Global Variable
description: Fetch the value of a global variable and populate a local argument

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description:
Enter the name of the global variable you would like to fetch

::warning
This is the **name** of the global variable, which means it should not be wrapped in `%` symbols unless you specifically want to use the **value** of a local variable in the **name** of your global variable
::

parameters.name: Destination Variable
parameters.description:
Enter the name of the local argument you would like to populate

::tip
It is recommended to use a **different name** than the global variable name
::

::warning
This is the **name** of the destination variable, which means it should not be wrapped in `%` symbols unless you specifically want to use the **value** of a local variable in the **name** of your destination variable
::

parameters.name: Default Value
parameters.description:
Optionally set a default value

This will create the global variable if it does not exist and then set it to the given value

variables.name: <variableName>
variables.description: The variable name and type will be depend on the parameters you selected

title: Set Global Variable
description: Create or update the value of a global variable

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description:
Enter the name of the global variable you would like to create or update

::warning
`Variable Name` is the **name** of the global variable, which means it should not be wrapped in `%` symbols unless you specifically want to use the **value** of a local variable as the **name** of your global variable
::

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description:
Enter the value to assign to the global variable, depending on the selected type in the dropdown

All options support parsing of %arguments%
- `Value` - Enter any value
- `Increment` - Enter any `int`{lang=cs} to be added to the existing value
- `Decrement` - Enter any `int`{lang=cs} to be subtracted from the existing value

::tip
If left empty, `Increment` and `Decrement` default to 1. If the global variable did not already exist, it will add or subtract from 0
::

parameters.description: If `Type` is `Between`, you can use this setting to configure the min/max values

title: If / Else
description: Perform conditional logic based on variable contents
summary: !If-Else Flow Chart by pwnyy (Right-Click to open full size in a new tab)

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description:
The value to be tested. Performs full parsing of `%arguments%`{lang=cs}, `~persistedGlobals~`{lang=cs} and `$inlineFunctions()$`{lang=cs} ::warning Unlike earlier version of Streamer.bot, arguments **must** be enclosed in `%...%` in order for their values to be tested
If using `Does Not Exist`, you **must** use *only* the argument name without the `%`. ::

parameters.name: Operator
parameters.description:
Choose the type of test to perform on the selected variable - `Equals`: Check if the variable equals a given value - `Not Equals`: Check if the variable does not equal a given value - `Contains`: Check if the variable contains the given value - `Regex Match`: Use a Regular Expression to match variable contents - `Less Than`: Check if a numeric variable is less than a given value (does NOT include the set value) - `Greater Than`: Check if a numeric variable is greater than a given value (does NOT include the set value) - `Does Not Exist`: Check if the variable name is defined at all - `Equals (Ignore Case)`: Check if a string variable equals a given value, case insensitive - `Not Equals (Ignore Case)`: Check if a string variable does not equal a given value, case insensitive - `Is Null or Empty`: Check if a variable is `Null`{lang=cs} or empty ::tip **Regular Expressions (RegEx) are extremely powerful!**
:icon{name=i-mdi-chevron-right} You can use tools like [regex101](https://regex101.com) and [RegExr](https://regexr.com) to view and test your expressions. ::

parameters.name: Auto Type
Value
parameters.description:
Enter the value you would like to use for comparison.

This field is parsed, so may contain other `%arguments%`{lang=cs}, `~persistedGlobals~`{lang=cs} and `$inlineFunctions()$`

variables.name: match.count
variables.description:
Only when using "Regex Match" as the operator.

The number of match groups (named or unnamed) in the regex.

Note that this is always 1 more than the number of parentheses groups, as the entire match is implicitly group 0.

variables.name: match[<index>]
variables.description:
Only when using "Regex Match" as the operator.

The text matching each indexed capture group.

Index 0 is the entire matching text, and the first parenthesized group is 1.

variables.name: <groupName>
variables.description:
Only when using "Regex Match" as the operator.

When using named capture groups (e.g, `(?'points'\d+)` or `(?<points>\d+)` ), each named capture group becomes an argument containing the text of the match.

title: Switch
description: Allows for multi-way branching based on the value of an expression

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description: Enter the value for the left hand side of the comparison, this input supports full variable parsing and inline functions

parameters.name: Auto Type

title: While
description: Allow for looping of sub-actions

parameters.name: Input
parameters.description:
Enter the value for the left hand side of the comparison

This can contain other `%variables%`{lang=cs} as well as inline functions

parameters.name: Operator
parameters.description:
Choose the type of comparison to perform

- `Equals`: Check if the variable equals a given value
- `Not Equals`: Check if the variable does not equal a given value
- `Less Than`: Check if a numeric variable is less than a given value (does NOT include the set value)
- `Greater Than`: Check if a numeric variable is greater than a given value (does NOT include the set value)

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description:
Enter the value for the right hand side of the comparison

This can contain other `%variables%`{lang=cs} as well as inline functions

parameters.name: Auto Type
Ignore Case
parameters.description:
Whether or not the comparison will be performed without checking casing

For example:
- `yes`{lang=cs} would match `YES`{lang=cs} is it is checked
- `yes`{lang=cs} would not match `YES`{lang=cs} if it is not checked

title: Set Voice Control Command State
description: Voice Control Sub-Actions Reference
summary:
Overview
!sb-vc-state.png

To do this navigate to the Actions tab, next you can either create a new action or use an existing one.
Next in the Sub-Action's pane you will need to add this sub action so to do this we right-click in the Sub-Action pane next navigate the menus through the following Add Sub-Action the down the second menu to Voice Control the click on the Set Voice Control Command State action. Once selected a dialog box will appear like the one below.

!set-vc-state.png

title: Set Voice Control Command
description: Voice Control Sub-Actions Reference
summary:
Overview
With the Streamer.bot (0.18 +) you can now assign an action to a voice control command, then trigger this with a keyword or sentence that you say.
To do this you need to make sure you have streamer.bot listening to you on the correct input device. The microphone that you are going to use.

Pre-requirement
First open streamer.bot and then navigate the following tabs Voice Control then in the second row of tabs click Settings in this take provide you have a support speech recognition package installed it should be usable.
You will want the bot to start listening to you when you start the bot up so go ahead and enable the "Auto Start Listen" by checking the box just like the image below. Also in this tab on the bottom is the "Audio Input Device"
when you have done this be sure the bot is listening by click the **Start Listening** button. click Save button at the top.

!vc-setting-tab.png

title: EpocCam Set AR Lens
description: Enables or Disables the AR Lens from the drop-down menu

parameters.name: AR Lens
parameters.description:
Select Disable, or the desired lens from the drop-down menu

- `Disable`: Disables any currently set lens
- `Clear Lens`: Puts a clear lens over the selected camera input
- `Black and White`: Turns the selected camera input Black and White
- `Background Blur`: Blurs the background of the selected camera input
- `Green Screen`: Adds a virtual green screen to the background of the selected camera input
- `Flower`: Applies a flower filter effect to the selected camera input
- `Invert`: Flips all colors on the selected camera input to their opposite hue on the color wheel
- `Sepia`: Applies a sepia filter effect to the selected camera input
- `VHS`: Applies a VHS-like effect to the selected camera input
- `Vivid`: Applies a vivid filter effect to the selected camera input
- `Elderly`: Makes the person on the selected camera input appear to be elderly
- `Toddler`: Makes the person on the selected camera input appear to be a toddler
- `Cyber-Me`: 'Cartoonifies' the person on the selected camera input
- `Anime`: Makes the person on the selected camera input appear to be an anime character
- `Bald`: Makes the person on the selected camera input appear to not have any hair on top of their head
- `Cartoon 3D`: Similar to 'Cyber-Me', it 'Cartoonifies' the person on the selected camera input
- `Crying`: Makes the person on the selected camera input appear to have a crying face

title: Set Background
description: Modify the background image of a Stream Deck key or dial
summary: Explore the full Stream Deck Plugin Documentation

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description:
Image path or data used to update the background image of a Stream Deck button

Supports any of the formats shown below:
::code-group
  ```bash [Local Path]
  C:\Users\John\Pictures\image.png
  ```
  ```bash [URL]
  https://api.iconify.design/mdi:alert.svg?color=%236312b5
  ```
  ```bash [Data URI]
  data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHdpZHRoPSIxZW0iIGhlaWdodD0iMWVtIiB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgMjQgMjQiPjxwYXRoIGQ9Ik0xMyAxNGgtMlY5aDJtMCA5aC0ydi0yaDJNMSAyMWgyMkwxMiAyTDEgMjF6IiBmaWxsPSIjNjMxMmI1Ii8+PC9zdmc+
  ```
  ```html [SVG]
  <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="32" height="32" viewBox="0 0 24 24"><path d="M13 14h-2V9h2m0 9h-2v-2h2M1 21h22L12 2L1 21z" fill="#6312b5"/></svg>
  ```
::

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description:
Set the background color of the given button.

::note
When color is configured in addition to the image field, the image will be placed on top of the selected background color.
::

title: Basic Webhook
description: Post messages to a Discord channel with webhooks
summary:
If you have not already configured a Webhook for your Discord server, check out the
Discord configuration guide to learn how.

parameters.name: Webhook Name
parameters.description:
Enter a name for your Webhook

_Variables are not supported_

parameters.name: Username
parameters.default: Username configured in Discord Webhook settings
parameters.description: Set a custom username for the author of the resulting Discord message

parameters.name: Webhook URL
parameters.description:
Enter the Webhook URL copied from the [Discord webhook configuration](/guide/integrations/discord)

_Variables are not supported_

parameters.name: Content
parameters.description:
Enter the text content for your webhook message. Multiple lines and variables are supported.

::collapsible{name="details for pinging users or roles"}
1. Enable Developer Mode

    Enable developer mode in Discord by opening settings and navigating to `App Settings -> Advanced`. Then, enable the `Developer Mode` toggle.

2. User Pings

    Users can be pinged in the following format:

    `<@DISCORD_USER_ID>` e.g. `<@0123456789>`

    With developer mode enabled, you can right-click on any user in discord, and click `Copy ID` to obtain their user ID.

3. Role Pings

    Roles can be pinged in the following format:

    `<@&DISCORD_ROLE_ID>` e.g. `<@&0123456789>`

    With developer mode enabled, you can right-click on a role in the server settings area, and click `Copy ID` to obtain its ID.
::

parameters.name: Avatar URL
parameters.default: Avatar configured in Discord Webhook settings
parameters.description:
Set a custom avatar for the author of the resulting Discord message

For example, you could set it to a chat user's profile picture by using the [Get User Info for Target](/api/sub-actions/twitch/user/get-user-info-for-target) Sub-Action and then using the `%targetUserProfileImageUrl%` variable.

parameters.name: Text to Speech
parameters.description: Toggle to send the message with Discord TTS

variables.name: messageId
variables.description: Message Id of the Discord message

variables.description: The type of bounding box
variables.value: OBS_BOUNDS_STRETCH, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_INNER, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_OUTER, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_TO_WIDTH, OBS_BOUNDS_SCALE_TO_HEIGHT, OBS_BOUNDS_MAX_ONLY or OBS_BOUNDS_NONE

variables.name: props.bounds.x
variables.description: The width of the bounding box

variables.name: props.bounds.y
variables.description: The height of the bounding box

variables.name: props.crop.bottom
variables.description: The number of pixels cropped off the bottom of the source before scaling

variables.name: props.crop.left
variables.description: The number of pixels cropped off the left of the source before scaling

variables.name: props.crop.right
variables.description: The number of pixels cropped off the right of the source before scaling

variables.name: props.crop.top
variables.description: The number of pixels cropped off the top of the source before scaling

variables.name: props.height
variables.description: Scene item height (base source height multiplied by the vertical scaling factor)

variables.name: props.itemId
variables.description: The scene Item id

variables.name: props.locked
variables.description: If the source's transform is locked

variables.name: props.message-id
variables.description: The id of the message

variables.name: props.muted
variables.description: If the source is muted

variables.name: props.name
variables.description: Scene Item name

variables.name: props.position.alignment
variables.description: The point on the source that the item is manipulated from. The sum of 1=Left or 2=Right, and 4=Top or 8=Bottom, or omit to centre on that axis

variables.name: props.position.x
variables.description: The x position of the source from the left

variables.name: props.position.y
variables.description: The y position of the source from the top

variables.name: props.rotation
variables.description: The clockwise rotation of the item in degrees around the point of alignment

variables.name: props.scale.filter
variables.description: The scale filter of the source
variables.value: OBS_SCALE_DISABLE, OBS_SCALE_POINT, OBS_SCALE_BICUBIC, OBS_SCALE_BILINEAR, OBS_SCALE_LANCZOS or OBS_SCALE_AREA

variables.name: props.scale.x
variables.description: The x-scale factor of the source

variables.name: props.scale.y
variables.description: The y-scale factor of the source

variables.name: props.sourceHeight
variables.description: Base source (without scaling) of the source

variables.name: props.sourceWidth
variables.description: Base width (without scaling) of the source

variables.name: props.status
variables.description: The status of the sub-action

variables.name: props.visible
variables.description: If the source is visible

variables.name: props.width
variables.description: Scene item width (base source width multiplied by the horizontal scaling factor)

variables.name: props.groupChildren[#].<one of these above>

variables.description: Everything above in a json format

Color / OBS Color
The Color picker will give the standard pallet picker, so you can choose by a slider or by entering raw RGB / HSL values

Once a color is chosen the Color box will be populated with the RGB Hex value and OBS Color will populate with the raw integer value OBS expects for ABGR it uses for its natively supported sources.

summary: :wip

title: Pin Chat Message
name: Pin Chat Message
description: Pins the provided message

parameters.name: Message Id
parameters.description: Enter the ID of the message to pin

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description:
The duration to pin the chat message for.
- `null` for the pin to last till the end of the stream
- between `30` and `1800` - duration in seconds

title: Reply to Message
description: Send a reply to a specific Twitch chat message

parameters.name: Reply Id
parameters.description:
Enter the unique ID of the message to reply to

::tip
All triggers that contain the `Twitch Chat` variables, will populate the `%msgId%`{lang=cs} variable
::

parameters.name: Message
parameters.description: Enter the message contents

parameters.name: Send using bot account
parameters.description:
- [x] Send the reply using your **Twitch Bot** account
- [ ] Send the reply using your **Twitch Broadcaster** account

parameters.name: Fallback to Broadcaster
parameters.description:
- [x] If `Send using Bot account` is **checked**, it will attempt to send reply as Twitch Bot account and, if unable, then send as Twitch Broadcaster.
- [ ] If `Send using Bot account` is **unchecked**, it will attempt to send reply as Twitch Bot account and, if unable, then do **nothing** (i.e. the Twitch Bot account is not logged in.

title: Send Announcement to Channel
description: Send an announcement to your Twitch channel

parameters.name: Preferred Account
parameters.description:
Select the Twitch account to use when sending the announcement

- `Bot`: Send the message from your [bot account](/guide/platforms/twitch#accounts)
- `Broadcaster`: Send the announcement from your [broadcaster account](/guide/platforms/twitch#accounts)

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description:
Select the color for the announcement

Options: `Default`, `Blue`, `Green`, `Orange`, `Purple`

parameters.name: Message
parameters.description: Enter the text content for the announcement

title: Send Message
description: Send a Twitch chat message

parameters.name: Message
parameters.description: Enter the message contents

parameters.name: Send using bot account
parameters.description:
- [x] Send the reply using your **Twitch Bot** account
- [ ] Send the reply using your **Twitch Broadcaster** account

parameters.name: Fallback to Broadcaster
parameters.description:
- [x] If `Send using Bot account` is **checked**, it will attempt to send reply as Twitch Bot account and, if unable, then send as Twitch Broadcaster.
- [ ] If `Send using Bot account` is **unchecked**, it will attempt to send reply as Twitch Bot account and, if unable, then do **nothing** (i.e. the Twitch Bot account is not logged in.

title: Get Follow Age Info for Target
description: Get the follow age from a Twitch user
summary:
C# Usage
:csharp-method

parameters.name: Source Type
parameters.description:
Select from which source you want to get the follow age:
- `User`: User that invoked the action e.g. a raid leader, subscriber, point redeemer etc.
- `From Input`: This will take the next word proceeding the trigger as the username to lookup. This user does not have to be present in the channel
- `Variable`: Use the content of an existing variable as the target

parameters.name: Variable
parameters.description: Argument name if `Source Type` is `Variable`

variables.name: isFollowing
variables.description: Whether the user is following the channel

variables.name: followDate
variables.description: The date the user followed

variables.name: followAgeLong
variables.description: How long the user has been following in a long format
variables.value: 1 year, 10 months, 5 days, 22 hours, 3 minutes, 25 seconds

variables.name: followAgeShort
variables.description: How long the user has been following in a short format

variables.name: followAgeDays
variables.description: The total number of days the user has been following

variables.name: followAgeMinutes
variables.description: The total number of minutes the user has been following

variables.name: followAgeSeconds
variables.description: The total number of seconds the user has been following

variables.name: followUser
variables.description: The users display name

variables.name: followUserName
variables.description: The user's login name

variables.name: followUserId
variables.description: The user's Twitch id

variables.name: followAge.years
variables.description: Amount of years followed

variables.name: followAge.months
variables.description: Amount of months followed (rest from years)

variables.name: followAge.days
variables.description: Amount of days followed (rest from months)

variables.name: followAge.hours
variables.description: Amount of hours followed (rest from days)

variables.name: followAge.minutes
variables.description: Amount of minutes followed (rest from hours)

variables.name: followAge.seconds
variables.description: Amount of seconds followed (rest from minutes)

title: Send Shoutout
description: Send a shoutout to a user on Twitch

parameters.name: User Login
parameters.description: User login name or a variable like `%userName%`

variables.name: shoutoutSuccess
variables.description:
- `True` - Shoutout was successfully made
- `False` - Shoutout failed
Reasons why a shoutout can fail:
- You, the broadcaster, is currently not live
- A shoutout is currently still on-going. 2 minutes per shoutout
- A shoutout was already done for the specified person in the last hour

title: Timeout User
description: Timeout a user
summary:
**Moderators**
Timing out a moderator will automatically reassign their moderator status once the timeout period ends. Editors will not receive their editior rights back.

This only applies to the subaction.

The C# method does not reassign their moderator status. You'll need to implement your own logic in C# to handle this.

parameters.name: User Login
parameters.description: Can be a user name or a variable like `%userName%`

parameters.name: Duration
parameters.description: Timeout duration in seconds. `0` is a permanent ban.

parameters.name: Reason
parameters.description: Reason for the timeout

variables.name: timedOutUser
variables.description: display name of the timed out user

variables.name: timedOutUserName
variables.description: login name of the timed out user

variables.name: timedOutUserId
variables.description: ID of the timed out user

variables.name: timedOutUserType
variables.description: platform of the timed out user

variables.name: timeoutResult
variables.description: indicator whether the timeout has been successful or not

latestSubscriber.userLogin | The latest subscriber's login name

:csharp-method

title: Get Team Info For Target
description: Twitch Sub-Actions Reference
summary:
Overview
Collects follow data from a Twitch user.

Configuration
Source Type
Name | Description
----:|:------------
Broadcaster | The broadcast user.
User | User that invoked the action e.g. a raid leader, subscriber, point redeemer etc.
From Input | This will take the next word proceeding the trigger as the username to lookup. This user does not have to be present in the channel
Variable | Use the content of an existing variable as the target

Variable
If you selected Variable as your Source Type, enter the name of the variable you would like to read in.

title: Get User Info for Target
description: Fetch Twitch user data from a given value and populate a set of variables
summary:
Parameters

The User Login field can contain either a literal username or a variable/argument.

**Examples**
- %userName% – The user who triggered the action
- genericuser – A specific account with the name genericuser
- %input0% – The first input provided (e.g., in a command or reward)
- %broadcastUserName% – The broadcaster’s account. If not used with a Twitch/Command trigger, be sure to add the Add Broadcaster Information sub-action first

summary:
Check out the Commands Guide for details on configuring your Commands

Parameters

Select a command from the Commands tab.

variables.name: command
variables.description: The command that was used

variables.name: commandId
variables.description: The ID of the command

variables.name: commandName
variables.description: The name of the command

variables.name: commandSource
variables.description: The command source

variables.name: commandType
variables.description: The type of the command

variables.name: msgId
variables.description: The unique message id for this message

variables.name: rawInput
variables.description: The message entered, if the command was a Starts With, this will be removed

variables.name: rawInputEscaped
variables.description: The message escaped

variables.name: rawInputUrlEncoded
variables.description: The message URL encoded

variables.name: input#
variables.description: The `#` word of the message entered, spaces are delimiters and variable names are 0 indexed, so `input0` would give the first word, `input1` would give the second, and so on

variables.name: inputEscaped#
variables.description: The indexed word escaped

variables.name: inputUrlEncoded#
variables.description: The indexed word URL encoded

variables.name: counter
variables.description: A running total of how many times a command has been run since application launch

variables.name: userCounter
variables.description: A running total of how many times the command has been run by current user since application launch

variables.name: match.count
variables.description:
Only when the command mode = "Regex"

The number of match groups (named or unnamed) in the regex.

Note that this is always 1 more than the number of parentheses groups, as the entire match is implicitly group 0.

variables.name: match[<index>]
variables.description:
Only when the command mode = "Regex"

The text matching each indexed capture group.

Index 0 is the entire matching text, and the first parenthesized group is 1.

variables.name: <groupName>
variables.description:
Only when the command mode = "Regex"

When using named capture groups (e.g, `(?'points'\d+)` or `(?<points>\d+)` ), each named capture group becomes an argument containing the text of the match.

parameters.name: Persisted
parameters.description:
- `Either` - Trigger on **both** persisted and non-persisted variables
- `Yes` - Trigger only on **persisted** variables
- `No` - Trigger only on **non-persisted** variables

parameters.name: User Login
parameters.description:
Enter the username of a specific user

Leave empty to trigger on **any** username

parameters.name: Platform
parameters.description:
- `Any` - Trigger on variable updates for **all** platforms
- `Twitch` - Only trigger on **Twitch** user variable updates
- `VStream` - Only trigger on **VStream** user variable updates
- `YouTube` - Only trigger on **YouTube** user variable updates

title: Custom Code Event
description: Trigger for a Custom Code Event
summary:
Details
This event can **only** be triggered with the CPH.TriggerCodeEvent C# Method. You can use two methods. With the first method you can use the variables of the current action. With the second method you can specify a dictionary of variables for the trigger.

When registering a trigger your trigger will end up in the Custom folder under triggers. You can nest the trigger in multiple folders by using the catergories parameter. To give the trigger a name use the triggerName parameter. Use the eventName parameter for a name only used in C# for triggering the method.

Variables
Variables are dependent on the custom event.

title: Custom
description: Trigger for a Custom Event
summary:
Details
This event can **only** be triggered with the CPH.TriggerEvent C# Method.

Variables
Variables are dependent on the custom event.

parameters.name: Event Name
parameters.description:
Input the event name of the custom event

- Leaving this blank is considered a catch-all and will trigger on **any** custom event

title: Coin Exchange
description: When a CrowdControl user exchanges for CrowdControl's Coin currency

parameters.name: Coin Type
parameters.description:
- `Any`
- `Free`
- `Paid`

parameters.name: Exchange Source
parameters.description:
- `Any`
- `Paypal`
- `Stripe`
- `Tiltify`
- `Twitch Bits`/`Twitch Channel Points`/`Twitch Sub`

parameters.name: Amount
parameters.description:
Filter events between a specified `Min` and `Max`

`Min` and `Max` are **inclusive** of the entered values

- `Any Value` - Leave both min and max empty to accept any value
- `Exact Value` - Enter only a `Max` to trigger on a specific value
- `Greater Than` - Enter only a `Min` to trigger on any value greater than the specified value
- `Range` - Enter a min and a max to trigger within a desired range

variables.name: coinType
variables.description: The type

variables.name: exchangeSource
variables.description: The source of the exchange

variables.name: anonymous
variables.description: If it was an anoymous exchange

variables.name: bonus
variables.description: Amount of bonus coins

title: Remote Instance Trigger
description: Triggers when a remote Streamer.bot instance trigger gets sent
summary:
This trigger is executed by the Send Remote Trigger sub-action, sent from another instance.

Additional variables will be populated for all arguments configured in the sending sub-action

parameters.name: Instance
parameters.description:
Select the instance to send this trigger to

- `Any` will trigger *all* connected instances

::tip{to=/guide/integrations/streamerbot}
All instances must have the [Streamer.bot Website Integration](/guide/integrations/streamerbot) configured and enabled
::

parameters.name: Event Name
parameters.description:
Name of the event to trigger on

- Leave blank to trigger on **any** incoming events
- To trigger on a specific event, enter the same name configured in the [Send Remote Trigger](/api/triggers/integrations/streamerbot-remote/remote-instance-trigger) sub-action on another instance

variables.name: instanceId
variables.description: The instance ID

variables.name: instanceName
variables.description: Name of the instance

variables.name: instanceVersion
variables.description: Streamer.bot version of the instance

variables.name: instanceOs
variables.description: The operation system of the instance

title: First Words
description: Trigger for a Kick First Words
summary:
Details
This triggers when someone sends their first message of the stream.

The exact reset time is set in Platforms -> Kick -> First Words and is 12h by default. That means that Streamer.bot must have been closed for 12 hours in order to reset the First Words trigger.

If you want to reset the First Words trigger, you can do so by going to Platforms -> Kick -> First Words and clicking the Reset button. This will reset the First Words trigger and allow it to be triggered again.

variables.name: firstMessage
variables.description: indicator whether the user's message was the first message **ever** in the channel

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for the Kick present viewers event
summary:
Notes
The Kick present viewer acts more like a chat activity tracker. There is no "Live-Update" option for Kick as the API does not provide the viewer list.

Under the Platforms > Kick > Settings, you will find the options for the Present Viewers Trigger.

The slider behaves as a threshold. The timer runs every minute, and checks the current time minus the user's last active time, if this is less then the threshold, they are marked as present, otherwise they are marked as not present.  The trigger will be executed every minute.

title: Ad Mid-Roll
description: Trigger for a Twitch Ad Mid-Roll
summary:
**Removed in Streamer.bot v1.0.0**
Twitch has deprecated the PubSub API and all triggers relying on it have been removed. It is **not recommended** to rely on this trigger. - This trigger utilizes an **undocumented and unsupported** Twitch PubSub event which could be removed, without warning, in the future. This event triggers 5 seconds prior to the start of an ad.

title: Automatic Reward Redemption
description: Trigger for when default channel point rewards or Power-ups have been used
summary:
Details
The Automatic Reward Redemptions work for both **default rewards** as well as the newly introduced **Power-ups**.

Default Rewards
The default rewards are those made by Twitch like "Highlight My Message". You can either choose to enable or disable them, but you cannot delete and only restrictively edit them.

!Default Rewards

parameters.name: Reward
parameters.description: Select any or a specific automatic channel reward type.

variables.name: rewardType
variables.description:
Type of the reward

Possible values: `send_highlighted_message`, `random_sub_emote_unlock`, `chosen_sub_emote_unlock`, `chosen_modified_sub_emote_unlock`, `single_message_bypass_sub_mode`, `message_effect`, `gigantify_an_emote`, `celebration`

variables.name: rewardCost
variables.description: channel point cost of the reward

variables.name: unlockedEmoteId
variables.description: ID of the unlocked emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: unlockedEmoteName
variables.description: name of the unlocked emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: userInput
variables.description: the input message of the redeeming user (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: gigantifiedEmoteId
variables.description: the ID of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.name: gigantifiedEmoteName
variables.description: the name of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

variables.description: the image URL of the gigantified emote (not available for all reward types)

title: Bot Whispers
description: Trigger for a Twitch Bot Whisper
summary:
Notes
The user sending the whisper must have a **verified phone number** (see the **Phone Number setting** in your **Security and Privacy settings**).

The API may silently drop whispers that it suspects of violating Twitch policies, it will still indicate success even if the message is dropped.

Rate Limits: You may whisper to a maximum of **40 unique recipients per day**. Within the per day limit, you may whisper a maximum of 3 whispers per second and a maximum of 100 whispers per minute.

title: First Words
description: Trigger for a Twitch First Words
summary:
This triggers when someone sends their first message of the stream.

The exact reset time is set in Platforms -> Twitch -> Settings and is 12h by default. That means that StreamerBot must have been closed for 12 hours in order to reset the First Words trigger.

You can create a new action, then create a  Settings -> Reset First Words subaction and assign a Twitch -> Channel -> Stream Online trigger. This will automatically reset the First Words trigger once you go live without any time requirement.

parameters.name: User Name
parameters.description: If you want to filter on a user name, you can enter a user name in this field

parameters.description: Check this field if the text in the `User Name` field is a user id

title: Whispers
description: Trigger for a Twitch Whisper
summary:
Notes
The user sending the whisper must have a **verified phone number** (see the **Phone Number setting** in your **Security and Privacy settings**).

The API may silently drop whispers that it suspects of violating Twitch policies, it will still indicate success even if the message is dropped.

Rate Limits: You may whisper to a maximum of **40 unique recipients per day**. Within the per day limit, you may whisper a maximum of 3 whispers per second and a maximum of 100 whispers per minute.

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for the Twitch present viewers event
summary:
Details
This triggers every 1-10 minutes, default every 5 minutes.

Notes
The present viewer tick will always happen, but you have the ability to have it "live update" from Twitch, or artificially mark someone as present.

Under the Platforms > Twitch > Settings, in the Present Viewers section, there are 2 settings, a Live Update check box, and a slider bar.

1=`Viewer`, 2=`VIP`, 3=`Moderator`, 4=`Broadcaster`

children.name: Type

children.description: On which letter the emote started

children.name: EndIndex
children.description: On which letter the emote ended

children.name: ImageUrl

title: Gift Subscription
description: Trigger for a Single Twitch Gift Subscription

parameters.name: Tier
parameters.description:
Choose on which tier to trigger the gift bomb

Options: `Tier 1`, `Tier 2`, `Tier 3`

parameters.name: Sub Type
parameters.description:
Choose on which sub types to trigger the gift bomb

Options: `Public`, `Anonymous`

parameters.name: Months Gifted
parameters.description:
Choose on how much months gifted to trigger the gift sub

Options: `1`, `3`, `6`, `12`

parameters.name: Milestone Range Min/Max
parameters.description: Limits the trigger to a range of total subscriptions gifted to the channel by this user. Leave blank to allow any minimum or maximum.

variables.description: The recipient user's display name

variables.description: The total amount of subscriptions that the user has gifted

`1`, `3`, `6`, `12`

variables.name: fromGiftBomb
variables.description: Is this from a gift bomb?

variables.name: subBombCount
variables.description: The amount of gift subs in the gift bomb

variables.name: cumulativeMonths
variables.description: The amount of months the recipient has been subscribed to the channel

variables.name: anonymous
variables.description: Is the gift sub anonymous?

variables.name: tier
variables.description:
The subscription tier
`tier 1`, `tier 2`, `tier 3`

variables.name: random
variables.description: Whether or not this was a targeted Gift sub, this will only be true for single gift subs that were directly given to a user.

variables.description: The system message that was put into Twitch Chat.
variables.value: Here's another gifted sub for your channel!

variables.description: A boolean value if the user gifting the sub(s) shares there total gift count.

title: Present Viewers
description: Trigger for YouTube viewer changes
summary:
Notes
The YouTube present viewer acts more like a chat activity tracker. There is no "Live-Update" option for YouTube as the API does not provide the viewer list.

Under the Platforms > YouTube > Settings, you will find the options for the Present Viewers Trigger.

The slider behaves as a threshold. The timer runs every minute, and checks the current time minus the user's last active time, if this is less then the threshold, they are marked as present, otherwise they are marked as not present.  The trigger will be executed every minute.

title: Visual Studio Code
description: Configure Visual Studio Code for writing C# code for Streamer.bot with linting and autocomplete.
headings: Create a new Streamer.bot Project Folder
summary:
**VSCode Extension now available!** :br
This extension automates the process of setting up a Streamer.bot C# project directory with IntelliSense, while also providing hover documentation for CPH methods. Installation includes the base C# extension dependency.

Write Streamer.bot C# code with IntelliSense and documentation

<small>by FajitaOfTreason</small>

codeBlocks.label: StreamerBot.csproj
codeBlocks.code:
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net481</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>13.0</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <UseWPF>true</UseWPF>
    <DefineConstants>EXTERNAL_EDITOR</DefineConstants>
    <NoWarn>CS0114</NoWarn>

    <!-- Set the following directory with your Streamer.bot install location -->
    <StreamerBotPath>C:/path/to/streamer.bot-directory</StreamerBotPath>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Automatically include in every CS file -->
    <Using Include="Streamer.bot.Plugin.Interface" />
    <Using Include="Streamer.bot.Plugin.Interface.Model" />
    <Using Include="Streamer.bot.Plugin.Interface.Enums" />
    <Using Include="Streamer.bot.Common.Events" />

    <!-- Use forward slashes for cross-platform compatibility -->
    <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/Streamer.bot.Plugin.Interface.dll" />
    <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/Streamer.bot.Common.dll" />
    <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/Twitch.Common.dll" />

    <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/NAudio*.dll" />
    <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/Wpf*.dll" />
    <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/Newtonsoft.Json.dll" />
    <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/System.*.dll" />

    <Reference Include="System.Web" />
    <Reference Include="System.Net.Http" />

    <!-- Uncomment the following line to reference all DLLs in the streamerbot directory -->
    <!-- <Reference Include="$(StreamerBotPath)/**/*.dll" /> -->
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

codeBlocks.label: UniqueClassName.cs

codeBlocks.code:
{
  "Execute C# Sub-Action Template": {
    "scope": "csharp",
    "isFileTemplate": true,
    "prefix": "streamer.bot-file-template",
    "description": "New Execute C# Sub-Action for Streamer.bot",
    "body": [
      "using System;",
      "",
      "/*----- Class name should match FileName -----*/",
      "#if EXTERNAL_EDITOR",
      "public class ${TM_FILENAME_BASE} : CPHInlineBase",
      "#else",
      "public class CPHInline",
      "#endif",
      "/*--------------------------------------------*/",
      "{",
      "    public bool Execute()",
      "    {",
      "        ${0:// Add your code here}",
      "        return true;",
      "    }",
      "}"
    ]
  }
}

title: SendAction
name: SendAction
description: Sends an Action from either the Twitch Broadcaster or Twitch Bot Account (denoted by *Italics* in Twitch chat)

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Enter the name of the Action exactly as it appears in Streamer.bot

parameters.name: bot
parameters.description:
- `true`{lang=cs} - Send the Action using your **Twitch Bot** account
- `false`{lang=cs} - Send the Action using your **Twitch Broadcaster** account

parameters.name: fallback
parameters.description:
- `true`{lang=cs} - (If `bot` bool is set to `True`), this is the same behaviour as if you had Bot as your preferred account.
- `false`{lang=cs} - (If `bot` bool is set to `True`), it will try to send using **only** the Bot account, and do **nothing** if it can't (i.e, not logged in).

title: SendWhisper
name: SendWhisper
description:
Send a Twitch Whipser to another user.

There are Twich limitations you have to consider before using this method:
- The user sending the whisper must have a verified phone number.
- The API may silently drop whispers that it suspects of violating Twitch policies.
- Rate Limits: You may whisper to a maximum of 40 unique recipients per day. Within the per day limit, you may whisper a maximum of 3 whispers per second and a maximum of 100 whispers per minute.

parameters.name: userName
parameters.description: Login name of the user you want to send a whisper to

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Enter the message contents

parameters.name: bot
parameters.description:
- `true`{lang=cs} - Send the message using your **Twitch Bot** account
- `false`{lang=cs} - Send the message using your **Twitch Broadcaster** account

title: Configuration
description: How to configure the Streamer.bot WebSocket Server

title: Using the Client
description: Connect to the Streamer.bot WebSocket Server using the official client package
summary:
The easiest way to interact with the WebSocket Server is by using the official client package

Installation
Install the package via your preferred package manager:

Or import it directly in the browser via a CDN:

codeBlocks.label: pnpm
codeBlocks.code: pnpm install @streamerbot/client

codeBlocks.label: npm
codeBlocks.code: npm install @streamerbot/client

codeBlocks.label: yarn
codeBlocks.code: yarn add @streamerbot/client

codeBlocks.label: index.html
codeBlocks.code: <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@streamerbot/client/dist/streamerbot-client.js"></script>

codeBlocks.label: index.html
codeBlocks.code: <script type="module" src="index.js"></script>

codeBlocks.label: index.js
codeBlocks.code: import { StreamerbotClient } from 'https://cdn.skypack.dev/@streamerbot/client';
const client = new StreamerbotClient();

title: Sending Requests
description: Learn how to send requests to the WebSocket server
summary:
Check out the **Streamer.bot Client** library if you are using JavaScript or TypeScript!

Request Format

Requests can be made to the server in stringified JSON format.

title: Receiving Events
description: Learn how to subscribe to and handle events from the WebSocket server
summary:
Subscribing to Events

In order to receive events from the WebSocket server, you must first subscribe to them.

When using the **Streamer.bot Client** library and its .on(){lang} method, this is handled automatically!

description: How to authenticate with the WebSocket server
summary:
Authentication was added to the WebSocket server in Streamer.bot v0.2.5.

Authentication is **optional** and can be enabled or disabled in the WebSocket settings.
- SendMessage is the only request where authentication is required.

title: Remote Access
description: Access your Streamer.bot instance from other devices
summary:
When interacting with the Streamer.bot WebSocket Server, you may want to access your instance from other devices, such as your phone or another computer.

Depending on your use case, there are a few different ways to achieve this.

Local Area Network (LAN)
You can access your Streamer.bot instance over your local network by simply using the local IP address of the machine running Streamer.bot.

codeBlocks.label: Terminal
codeBlocks.code: tailscale serve 8080

title: WebSocket Requests
description: Reference of all requests that can be made to the Streamer.bot WebSocket Server
summary:
Check out the **Streamer.bot Client** library if you are using JavaScript or TypeScript!

Requests can be made to the server in stringified JSON format.

Requests and responses follow the following base schema:

title: Chatbot Commands
description: Configure some basic chat commands with Streamer.bot
summary:
Overview

If you are coming from another bot platform, you are probably used to having some basic commands available out of the box.

**Streamer.bot ships with a blank slate**, but a few simple actions can provide similar functionality.

title: Simple Counter
description: Create a simple counter to count up or down
summary:
Counters can be used in various ways, for example:
- Death counters in video games
- Counting how many times the streamer has yawned
- Keep track of how many times a specific command has been used.

While commands and Twitch Channel Point Rewards offer their own built-in counters, they're not as versatile as the following example which utilizes **global variables**.
:read-more{to="/guide/variables#global-variables"}

!Counter Preview

title: OBS Raw Requests in C#
description: An example how to send OBS Raw requests and work with the responses in C#
summary:
You are probably already familiar with sending OBS Raw requests with Streamer.bot's OBS Raw Generator or the Raw subaction that you find under OBS -> Raw. Sending it vía the subaction then populates variables that you can further use in your action. Sending Raw requests in C# however works a little different.

Instructions

1. Retrieving the CPH method

codeBlocks.code: CPH.ObsSendRaw("GetInputSettings", "{\"inputName\":\"Bitrate Text\"}", 0);
string gdiText = (string)inputSettingsJObject["inputSettings"]["text"];
int fontSize = (int)inputSettingsJObject["inputSettings"]["font"]["size"];
CPH.LogInfo($"GDI Text: {gdiText} // Font Size: {fontSize}");
[2024-08-11 19:13:48.297 INF] GDI Text: Current bitrate: 7500 // Font Size: 144
CPH.ObsSendRaw("SetInputSettings", "{\"inputName\":\"Other Textsource\",\"inputSettings\":{\"font\":{\"size\":144},\"text\":\"7500\"},\"overlay\":true}", 0);

title: cURL POST Requests
description: Example code for making POST requests with cURL in Streamer.bot
summary:
While the built-in Fetch URL sub-action is great for making quick HTTP GET requests, sometimes you may need to make more complex requests, such as POST requests with custom headers and body content. This utility action provides a simple way to make HTTP requests using cURL, which supports a wide range of HTTP methods and options. curl is now included by default on Windows 10 or later, so there is no need to install it separately.
This utility action will use the system curl executable to make HTTP requests.

title: Advanced "Fetch URL"
description: Send web requests manually using HTTPClient.
summary:
StreamerBot already has the built-in Fetch URL sub-action to immediately send simple HTTP GET requests directly from the actions UI.

Sometimes, however, you will find a situation where you need to send data via POST, PUT or generally any other request type that is *not* GET.

In this case, you will need to implement your own request handling in a C# code module using the HttpClient{target=_blank rel=noopener} class.

title: AI Chat Command
description: Create a basic AI chat command with Ollama and Python
summary:
You can utilize the Run a Program sub-action to execute scripts with Streamer.bot :read-more{to="/api/sub-actions/core/system/run-a-program"} **Requires Streamer.bot v0.2.5-beta.2 or later**
A bug in previous versions of Streamer.bot prevented capturing of stdout from scripts

codeBlocks.label: Command Prompt
codeBlocks.code: ollama run llama3.1

codeBlocks.label: Streamer.bot\data\scripts\ollama-example.py
codeBlocks.code:
import sys
    import ollama

    # Load the prompt from command line arguments
    prompt = " ".join(sys.argv[1:])

    # Generate text using llama3.1 model
    response = ollama.generate(model="llama3.1", prompt=prompt)

    # Print the response as a single line, truncated at 500 characters
    output = response["response"].replace("\n", " ")
    print(output[0:499])

title: Remote Triggers
description: Easily communicate between multiple Streamer.bot instances
summary:
You can use **Remote Triggers** to easily send data or execute actions on **multiple** Streamer.bot instances. It even works over remote networks!

To **send** events to another instance, we utilize the **Send Remote Trigger** sub-action

To **receive** events on another instance, we uitlize the **Remote Instance Trigger**

title: Streamer.bot Starter Pack
description: Get started with a collection of starter extensions provided by the community!
summary:
Introduction

This is a starter pack for anyone wanting to get started using Streamer.bot. It contains a wide range of extensions for you to use, including alerts, a point system, a death counter and many other features.

This package was built as a part of a raid train streamed live on Twitch, in celebration of Streamer.bot's 3rd birthday on August 24, 2024.

summary:
First off, Happy Birthday ChannelPointsHandler, you turned 1 on June 16th 2021, and as a celebration, a massive update, packed with new features and fixes all around.

Thank you everyone for deciding to use this piece of software that I write in my spare time, and sometimes, don't update for months 😄 This community has been something special, and is very dear to my heart.  The VR community at large has had such an impact on me that I can't begin to thank you all enough for. From landing a job at a VR company (LIV), to finding that someone special in my life (Lithaya, if I may, I'd like to dedicate this release to you), and for many great friends along the way.

My DMs are always open, and, I don't bite (well, I try not to); while I may seem like I'm super busy all the time, you might be surprised, I do like playing games or anything else at times to 😄

Since I forgot to update the Twitch client keys in the initial release, this version will request a re-authorization to twitch.

* Miscellaneous fixes
* Hopefully final fix for BTTV/FFZ emotes
* Fix Action Queue UI not working
* Fix issues with Bot login
